summary: German opera composer (1714–1787), best known for his 'reform operas' which rejected Baroque convention in favor of drama and simplicity. Collaborated with librettist Ranieri de' Calzabigi on Orfeo ed Euridice (1762), Alceste (1767), and Paride ed Elena. The preface to Alceste laid out reform principles (no da capo arias, no secco recitatives, syllabic text-setting). Had a famous Paris rivalry with the Neapolitan Niccolò Piccinni. Was the music teacher of Marie Antoinette and the main operatic model for Berlioz.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: German opera composer who led the 18th-century 'reform opera' movement, working to prioritize drama over vocal display. Collaborated with librettist Ranieri de' Calzabigi. Music teacher of Marie Antoinette. Wagner cited him as a predecessor of the Gesamtkunstwerk. Main operatic model for Hector Berlioz. Suffered a stroke later in life; Antonio Salieri took over the composition of Les Danaïdes. Echo et Narcisse's poor reception caused him to retire to Vienna. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Reform opera composer who worked with librettist Ranieri de' Calzabigi to eliminate da capo arias, secco recitatives, and melismas; principles set out in preface to Alceste
examples: This composer collaborated with the librettist Calzabigi to get rid of plain recitatives, static da capo arias, and other 'overcomplicated' and 'undramatic' conventions, principles set out in the preface to Alceste.
A preface to one of this composer's operas advocates for syllabic rather than melismatic text-setting and for abandoning da capo form.

clues.clue: Music teacher of Marie Antoinette; composed Iphigénie en Aulide for her
examples: This music teacher of Marie Antoinette composed Iphigenie en Aulide.

clues.clue: Main operatic model for Hector Berlioz; his music comprises the majority of operatic excerpts in Berlioz's Treatise on Instrumentation; Berlioz rearranged one opera for an 1859 production with Pauline Viardot in the lead male role
examples: This composer's music comprises the majority of the operatic excerpts in the Treatise on Instrumentation by Hector Berlioz, who rearranged one of this composer's operas for an 1859 production, starring Pauline Viardot in the main male role.
This composer was the main operatic model for Hector Berlioz.

clues.clue: Wagner cited him as a predecessor of his idea of the Gesamtkunstwerk
examples: Wagner cited this composer as a predecessor of his own idea of the Gesamtkunstwerk because of the changes to operatic norms that this composer advocated in an essay.

clues.clue: Rivalry with Niccolò Piccinni in Paris; Parisians divided into 'Gluckists' and Piccinni fans; both commissioned to write rival settings of Iphigénie en Tauride
examples: This composer's rivalry with Niccolo Piccinni was so widely known and controversial that Parisians would open conversations by asking which side the other favoured.
Parisian operagoers were divided between 'Gluckists' and fans of this Neapolitan composer of La Cecchina. The Paris Opera commissioned him and Gluck to write rival settings of Iphigenie en Tauride.

clues.clue: Handel insulted him early on, saying that his cook knew more counterpoint than Gluck
examples: Early on, this composer was insulted when Handel claimed that his cook knew more counterpoint this man.

clues.clue: Echo et Narcisse's poor reception led to his retirement to Vienna
examples: The poor reception of Echo et Narcisse led to this composer's retirement to Vienna.

clues.clue: Suffered a stroke; Salieri took over the composition of Les Danaïdes on his behalf
examples: After this composer suffered a stroke, their protege Salieri took over the composition of Les Danaides.
He suffered a stroke and had Salieri take the commission [for Les Danaides] instead.

clues.clue: In E.T.A. Hoffmann's story 'Ritter Gluck,' a strange man claims to be this dead composer and sings an aria from blank pages
examples: In this Hoffmann story, a strange man from the Tiergarten relays a dream about a disembodied eye that promises the sweet voice of thirds. This story's title character sings an aria from blank pages then claims to be a dead composer.

works.name: Orfeo ed Euridice
works.description: Reform opera premiered in Vienna in 1762; a revised French version (Orphée et Eurydice) was premiered in Paris in 1774, when Gluck added the 'Dance of the Blessed Spirits.' The lead male role (Orfeo/Orphée) was sung by a castrato in the Vienna premiere. The most famous aria is 'Che farò senza Euridice' (What shall I do without Euridice). Act II features a dancer revealing Elysium. Wikipedia

clues.clue: 1762 reform opera; contains the 'Dance of the Blessed Spirits' (added to the French 1774 revision)
examples: An aria beginning with the words 'Che faro senza' appears in his 1762 'reform opera' that includes a 'Dance of the Blessed Spirits.'
The 'Dance of the Blessed Spirits' appears in an opera by this composer whose third act centers on an escape from Hades.

clues.clue: Most famous aria: 'Che farò senza Euridice' (What shall I do without Euridice)
examples: After the rondo 'Che faro senza,' Amore brings one of the title characters back to life in an opera by this composer.
This 18th-century German composer wrote the music for Alceste and Orfeo ed Euridice, the latter of which contains his most famous aria, 'Che faro senza Euridice.'

clues.clue: Lead male role sung by a castrato in the Vienna premiere; not in the French premiere
examples: The lead male role in this opera was sung by a castrato in its Viennese premiere, but not its reworked French premiere.

clues.clue: A dancer removes a scarf cover and reveals Elysium in Act II; the aria 'Che puro ciel' is sung by the title character upon arriving in Elysium
examples: A dancer removes a scarf cover and reveals Elysium in the second act of an opera by this composer that includes the aria 'Che puro ciel.'
A title character in another of this composer's operas sings the aria 'Che puro ciel' after arriving in Elysium.

clues.clue: Amore (Cupid) brings Euridice back to life; earlier convinces Orfeo not to commit suicide ('Tendre amour'); sets conditions for Orfeo's return in the trio 'Si les doux accords'
examples: Amore later convinces that character not to commit suicide after his failure in the aria 'Tendre amour.'
In this opera's trio 'Si les doux accords,' Amore sets out the conditions to a title character for returning successfully from the underworld.

clues.clue: Berlioz rearranged this opera in 1859 with Pauline Viardot in the lead male role; the oldest regularly performed opera is Monteverdi's L'Orfeo (on the same myth)
examples: Berlioz...rearranged one of this composer's operas for an 1859 production, starring Pauline Viardot in the main male role.

works.name: Alceste
works.description: 1767 reform opera; Gluck's second collaboration with Calzabigi. The preface/manifesto to the Italian version (probably co-authored by Calzabigi) decried da capo arias, secco recitatives, and 'tiresome ritornellos.' The title Queen of Thessaly sings 'Divinités du Styx' in Act I, resolving to die for her husband. Berlioz argued that Mozart plagiarized the Commendatore's music in Don Giovanni from this opera. Jean-Jacques Rousseau criticized the Italian version as monotonous. Mozart wrote an insert aria 'Popoli di Tessaglia!' for it. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Preface (probably co-authored with Calzabigi) decried da capo arias, secco recitatives, 'tiresome ritornellos'; overture should 'apprise the spectator of the action'
examples: This text notes a desire to avoid interrupting an 'actor in the greatest heat of dialogue' with a 'tiresome ritornello,' and claims that an overture 'ought to apprise the spectator of the action to be represented.'
This opera was first premiered with a preface by Ranieri de' Calzabigi with a list of reformatory maxims, including a ban on both melismas and improvisation.

clues.clue: Title character sings 'Divinités du Styx' — resolving to sacrifice herself to save her husband Admète
examples: The title character of an opera by this composer resolves to sacrifice herself to save her husband in the aria 'Divinites du Styx.'
Berlioz suggested that Mozart plagiarized some of the Commendatore's music in Don Giovanni from this opera, in which the title character sings 'Divinites du Styx.'

clues.clue: Berlioz argued that Mozart plagiarized the Commendatore's music in Don Giovanni from Alceste
examples: Berlioz suggested that Mozart plagiarized some of the Commendatore's music in Don Giovanni from this opera.

clues.clue: Jean-Jacques Rousseau criticized the Italian version as monotonous; the French version addressed his complaint by not putting all the best arias in Act I
examples: The French version of Gluck's Alceste addressed this critic's complaint that the Italian version was monotonous and put all the best arias in Act I.

clues.clue: Mozart wrote an insert aria 'Popoli di Tessaglia!' for it (an exceptionally high aria written for Aloysia Weber)
examples: Mozart wrote many pieces for her sister Aloysia, including the exceptionally high aria 'Popoli di Tessaglia!', an insert for this composer's opera Alceste.

works.name: Iphigénie en Tauride
works.description: Gluck's most developed reform opera, premiered 1779. Based on Euripides. Libretto by Nicolas-François Guillard. Features a scene where the orchestral accompaniment reveals that a character is lying. Opens with a short calm passage leading directly into a storm depiction. The Paris Opera commissioned both Gluck and Niccolò Piccinni to write rival settings; Gluck's version won. Includes a 'Dance of the Scythians' and the aria 'Je t'implore et je tremble.' Wikipedia

clues.clue: Gluck's most developed reform opera; opens with calm passage leading into a storm depiction; a scene has the orchestral accompaniment reveal that a character is lying
examples: Gluck's reforms were most extensive in this Euripides-based opera, which has a scene in which the orchestral accompaniment shows that the singer is lying. This opera opens with a short passage depicting calm turning into a storm.
Name this opera that swaps an overture for a short, calm passage preceding a more aggressive depiction of a storm at sea.

clues.clue: Gluck and Piccinni were commissioned to write rival settings; feud was driven by a split between the king's mistress and the dauphine
examples: The Paris Opera commissioned him [Piccinni] and Gluck to write rival settings of Iphigenie en Tauride.
A feud between the king of France's mistress and the dauphine led this composer and a Neapolitan rival to write competing settings of the same libretto.

clues.clue: Includes a 'Dance of the Scythians' and the aria 'Je t'implore et je tremble'; features the Scythian king Thoas
examples: The ballet section 'Dance of the Scythians' appears in an opera by this composer whose most performed aria was adapted from the aria 'Se mai senti...'
Nicolas-Francois Guillard wrote the libretto to this opera, which includes a part for King Thoas.

clues.clue: Gluck also wrote a German version for the visit of Grand Duke Paul
examples: Though originally written in French, Gluck wrote a version of Iphigenia in Tauris in this language [German] for the visit of Grand Duke Paul.

clues.clue: Most widely performed aria was adapted from the earlier aria 'Se mai senti spirarti sul volto' from his version of La clemenza di Tito
examples: The ballet section 'Dance of the Scythians' appears in an opera by this composer whose most performed aria was adapted from the aria 'Se mai senti spirarti sul volto' from his version of La clemenza di Tito.

works.name: Paride ed Elena
works.description: Third reform opera by Gluck and Calzabigi. Features the aria 'O del mio dolce ardor,' an unusual minor-key love song long misattributed to Alessandro Stradella. Cupid disguises himself as Erasto to convince Helen to love Paris. Janet Baker's last operatic performance was in this opera. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Third of three reform operas Gluck wrote with Calzabigi; features the aria 'O del mio dolce ardor,' long misattributed to Alessandro Stradella
examples: An unusual minor-key love song in an opera by this composer, 'O del mio dolce ardor,' was long misattributed to Alessandro Stradella.
Paride ed Elena is the third of three operas this composer wrote with librettist Ranieri de' Calzabigi.

clues.clue: Cupid disguises himself as Erasto to convince Helen to love Paris
examples: Cupid disguises himself as Erasto to convince Helen to love Paris in that opera, Paride ed Elena.
In this opera, Erasto turns out to be Cupid in disguise.

clues.clue: Janet Baker's last operatic performance was in this opera
examples: Janet Baker's last operatic appearance was as her signature role in an opera by this composer.

comprehensive_summary:
Christoph Willibald Gluck (1714–1787) was a German opera composer best known for leading the 18th-century 'reform opera' movement. Working with librettist Ranieri de' Calzabigi, he sought to make opera more dramatic and natural by eliminating the artificial conventions of Baroque opera seria: da capo arias, secco recitatives (speech-like passages with only keyboard accompaniment), and elaborate melismatic vocal display. His reform principles were set out in the preface to Alceste (1767), probably co-authored with Calzabigi, which decried 'tiresome ritornellos' and argued that an overture should 'apprise the spectator of the action to be represented.' Wagner later cited Gluck as a predecessor of his own idea of the Gesamtkunstwerk.

Gluck was the music teacher of Marie Antoinette and composed Iphigénie en Aulide for the French court. Early in his career, Handel insulted him by saying his cook knew more counterpoint. He became the principal operatic model for Hector Berlioz, whose Treatise on Instrumentation draws primarily on Gluck's operas. Berlioz rearranged Orfeo ed Euridice for an 1859 production with the mezzo-soprano Pauline Viardot in the lead male role. Gluck suffered a stroke later in life and handed the commission for Les Danaïdes to his protégé Antonio Salieri. The poor reception of Echo et Narcisse caused him to retire to Vienna.

Orfeo ed Euridice (1762) was his first 'reform opera.' The lead male role was sung by a castrato in the Vienna premiere; the revised French version (Orphée et Eurydice, 1774) added the 'Dance of the Blessed Spirits.' The most famous aria, 'Che farò senza Euridice,' is a rondo sung when Orfeo thinks Euridice is lost; the god Amore (Cupid) eventually brings her back to life, after first talking Orfeo out of suicide in the aria 'Tendre amour.' In Act II, a dancer removes a scarf cover to reveal Elysium; the aria 'Che puro ciel' is sung by the title character on arriving there.

Alceste (1767) centers on the Queen of Thessaly, who sings 'Divinités du Styx' in Act I, resolving to sacrifice herself to save her husband Admète. Berlioz argued that Mozart plagiarized the Commendatore's music in Don Giovanni from this opera. Jean-Jacques Rousseau criticized the Italian version as monotonous (putting all best arias in Act I); the French version addressed this. Mozart wrote an insert aria 'Popoli di Tessaglia!' for Alceste for the singer Aloysia Weber.

Iphigénie en Tauride (1779) was Gluck's most developed reform opera. It opens without a formal overture — instead, a short calm passage leads directly into a storm. In one scene, the orchestral accompaniment reveals that a character is lying. The Paris Opera commissioned both Gluck and Niccolò Piccinni to write rival settings of the same libretto (the rivalry was fueled by a political split between the king's mistress and the dauphine), dividing Parisian operagoers into 'Gluckists' and Piccinni fans. The opera features a 'Dance of the Scythians' and includes the role of the Scythian king Thoas. Gluck also wrote a German version for Grand Duke Paul's visit.

Paride ed Elena (the third reform opera with Calzabigi) features the aria 'O del mio dolce ardor,' an unusual minor-key love song long misattributed to Alessandro Stradella, and the plot twist that Erasto is Cupid in disguise. It was the vehicle for Janet Baker's farewell to the operatic stage.

Composer: Handel said his cook knew more counterpoint than him; music teacher of Marie Antoinette; primary operatic model for Berlioz

Composer: suffered a stroke and had Salieri take over the composition of Les Danaïdes; Echo et Narcisse's failure caused retirement to Vienna

Opera: 1762 reform opera; contains the 'Dance of the Blessed Spirits' (added in 1774 French revision); most famous aria is 'Che farò senza Euridice'; lead role was sung by a castrato in Vienna premiere
Opera: in Act II, a dancer removes a scarf to reveal Elysium; Amore talks the protagonist out of suicide in 'Tendre amour' and brings Euridice back to life

Opera: preface/manifesto (with Calzabigi) decried 'tiresome ritornellos' and said an overture should 'apprise the spectator of the action'; title character sings 'Divinités du Styx' resolving to die for her husband
Opera: Berlioz argued Mozart plagiarized the Commendatore's music in Don Giovanni from this opera; Rousseau criticized its Italian version as monotonous
Opera: most developed reform opera; opens with calm passage leading directly into a storm (no overture); a scene where the orchestral accompaniment reveals a character is lying
Opera: the Paris Opera commissioned both Gluck and Niccolò Piccinni to write rival settings; the feud was driven by a split between the king's mistress and the dauphine
